title: chenopodium album var. lanceolatum
intro: Chenopodium album var. lanceolatum, also known as lamb's quarters, is a variety of the lamb's quarters plant, a type of flowering plant in the goosefoot family (Chenopodiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of lamb's quarters include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to support immune function and overall well-being.
